description: How the War is Affecting Reform in Russia Douma Coalition Patty Refuses to Disperse and Calls for Governmental Changes Correspondence of the Sunday Journal PETROGRAD Oct 10 HOLY Russia is halfway toward revolutionrevolution peaceful so far hut broader more menacing and more hopeful than the abortive revo lution which followed the war with Japan The causes are the same the initial symp toms are the same the men are the same There is the same Czar with most of the same bureaucrats there is the same Liberal bourgeoise with all the old lead ers and catchwords and there is the same Black People The Black People the Tchernis the third party to the revolution The only differences are that j the first revolution did not want to beat the Japanese and the second revolution i does not want to beat the Germans and j that the first revolution never had a I chance of success whereas the second i revolution seems bound to accomplish something Russias second revolution is the fruit of military defeat No defeated State in this war can escape revolution Some j prophets say there will be revolution j everywherevictors will have it as much as vanquished
